zh.Breda, Noord-Brabant, Nederland: 荷蘭努-布拉班特布雷達
zh.lifeSummary: 當Heer Hendrik II van Breda en Schoten於大約1130年出生在荷蘭努-布拉班特時，他的父親Gerard Hendrik Reinier van Breda van Brunesheim Schoten11歲，母親Béatrix de la Roche33歲。他和Christina Van Schoten至少有3個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1179年在法國皮卡迪索姆享年50歲。

zh-hans.Breda, Noord-Brabant, Nederland: 荷兰努-布拉班特布雷達
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Heer Hendrik II van Breda en Schoten于大约1130年出生在荷兰努-布拉班特时，他的父亲Gerard Hendrik Reinier van Breda van Brunesheim Schoten11岁，母亲Béatrix de la Roche33岁。他和Christina Van Schoten至少有3个儿子和2个女儿。他去世于1179年在法国皮卡迪索姆享年50岁。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Breda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Aldo, Angelo, Dino, Domenic, Donato, Egidio, Geno, Massimo, Quintino, Sante, Ugo.

Italian: topographic name from medieval Latin braida ‘grassy meadow, flat grassland adjoining a settlement’, or a habitational name from any of the places called with this word, such as the districts (now suburbs) of Genoa, Milan, Mantua, and Brescia called Breda or Brera.
Dutch: habitational name fromthe city of this name in North Brabant.

nameMeanings.name: en
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 岑, referring to ‘small high hills’ or ‘precipice’ in Chinese: (i) from the placename Cen (岑), the name of a fief (located in Hancheng in present-day Shaanxi province), which was granted to Qu, nephew of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056 BC ), by King Wu of Zhou (c. 1087–1043 BC ). (ii) for some families, this surname can be traced back to certain minority ethnic groups in ancient southern China.

nameMeanings.name: Schoten
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Dirk, Bastian, Berend, Gradus, Harmen, Hendrick, Jacobus, Willem, Wim.
Dutch and German: patronymic from the status name Scholte ‘village headman’, a variant of Schulte .

variants.name: Schulte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Dieter, Heinz, Rainer, Alois, Dietmar, Franz, Fritz, Gerhard, Hans.

North German and Dutch: status name from Middle Low German schulthete, Middle Dutch schulte ‘village headman’. In the northwestern Germany the name denotes the holder of a large farm. His status came with the obligation to make certain legal decisions. Compare Schulz , Schultheis .

nameMeanings.name: Hendrik
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Henry
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

en.lifeSummary: When Heer Hendrik II van Breda en Schoten was born about 1130, in North Brabant, Netherlands, his father, Gerard Hendrik Reinier van Breda van Brunesheim Schoten, was 11 and his mother, Béatrix de la Roche, was 33. He had at least 3 sons and 2 daughters with Christina Van Schoten. He died in 1179, in Somme, Picardie, France, at the age of 50.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Heer Hendrik II van Breda en Schoten est né environ 1130, à Brabant-septentrional, Pays-Bas, son père, Gerard Hendrik Reinier van Breda van Brunesheim Schoten, avait 11 ans et sa mère, Béatrix de la Roche, avait 33 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles avec Christina Van Schoten. Il est décédé en 1179, à Somme, Picardie, France, à l’âge de 50 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Heer Hendrik II van Breda en Schoten um 1130 in Nord-Brabant, Niederlande geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gerard Hendrik Reinier van Breda van Brunesheim Schoten 11 und seine Mutter Béatrix de la Roche 33. Er hatte mit Christina Van Schoten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1179 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Somme, Picardie, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Heer Hendrik II van Breda en Schoten è nato il circa 1130 nel Brabante settentrionale, Paesi Bassi, il suo padre, Gerard Hendrik Reinier van Breda van Brunesheim Schoten, aveva 11 anni e il suo madre, Béatrix de la Roche, aveva 33 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie con Christina Van Schoten. Morì nel 1179, nel Somme, Picardie, Francia, all'età di 50 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Heer Hendrik II van Breda en Schotenが約1130年にオランダノオルド-ブラバントで生まれた時，父Gerard Hendrik Reinier van Breda van Brunesheim Schotenは11歳，母Béatrix de la Rocheは33歳であった。彼はChristina Van Schotenとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1179年にフランスピカーディソンメで50歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Heer Hendrik II van Breda en Schoten(이)가 대략 1130년 네덜란드 노르드-브라반트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gerard Hendrik Reinier van Breda van Brunesheim Schoten(은)는 11세였고, 그의 어머니 Béatrix de la Roche(은)는 33세였다. 그는 Christina Van Schoten(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1179년 프랑스 피카디 솜에서 50세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.Breda, Noord-Brabant, Nederland: Breda, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Heer Hendrik II van Breda en Schoten nasceu aproximadamente 1130, em Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos, seu pai, Gerard Hendrik Reinier van Breda van Brunesheim Schoten, tinha 11 anos e sua mãe, Béatrix de la Roche, tinha 33 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas com Christina Van Schoten. Ele faleceu em 1179, em Somme, Picardie, França, com 50 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Heer Hendrik II van Breda en Schoten родился около 1130 г. в местности нурд-брабант, Нидерланды, его отцу по имени Gerard Hendrik Reinier van Breda van Brunesheim Schoten было 11 лет, а матери, которую звали Béatrix de la Roche, 33. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери от Christina Van Schoten. Он умер в 1179 г. в местности сомм, пикарди, Франция в возрасте 50 лет.

es.Breda, Noord-Brabant, Nederland: Breda, Brabante del Norte, Países Bajos
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Heer Hendrik II van Breda en Schoten nació aproximadamente 1130, en Brabante del Norte, Países Bajos, su papá, Gerard Hendrik Reinier van Breda van Brunesheim Schoten, tenía 11 años y su mamá, Béatrix de la Roche, tenía 33 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas con Christina Van Schoten. Murió en 1179, en Somme, Picardie, Francia, a la edad de 50 años.
